inflation.label: oil + tariffs
inflation.note: DATED (Sept 2025, pre oil shock): inflation pickup driven by goods prices from tariffs, explicitly not broader pressures, with no oil/energy theme yet.
inflation.quote: Incoming data and surveys suggest that those price increases largely reflect higher tariffs rather than broader price pressures.

labor.label: downside risks
labor.note: Softer, less dynamic labor market with job gains slowing to ~29,000/month and downside risks to employment having risen.
labor.quote: In this less dynamic and somewhat softer labor market, the downside risks to employment have risen.

stance.label: somewhat/modestly restrictive
stance.note: After the September 25bp cut toward neutral he characterizes policy as still modestly restrictive and well positioned (read is dated, pre oil shock).
stance.quote: This policy stance, which I see as still modestly restrictive, leaves us well positioned to respond to potential economic developments.
